name: Villa Tabaiba
description: This kaleidoscopic house may be the most bizarre on the island of Fuerteventura.

name: The Hermitage of la Mare de Deu del Carme
description: The bell that resides above this small chapel is actually a converted bomb.

name: Casa de los Tiros
description: A centuries-old house, once part of the wall of Granada, sports musket barrels from the battlements which allude to its original owner.

name: Ibiza's Wall of Fame
description: An unexpected homage to 90s celebrities hidden in a corner of Ibiza.

name: Pont de Montiberri
description: A river hides a charming medieval bridge.

name: Spain's Oldest Postbox
description: The oldest functioning mailbox in Spain was installed in 1793.

name: Baltasar Bachero Tile Mosaic
description: Baltasar Bachero became a hero after he saved the lives of two children nearly run over by an out-of-control mule-drawn carriage.

name: Cascada de las Castañuelas
description: This waterfall, also known as the “the castanets” is said to mimic the sounds of the waves in Portugal.

name: Museo de Esculturas
description: An open-air sculpture museum in a park in Alcorcón.

name: Puente Romano
description: A Roman bridge closed to car traffic in the 1990s now open to foot traffic.

name: Parque Natural de las Dunas de Corralejo
description: Park in the Canary Islands renowned for white dunes that contrast with the turquoise waters of the Atlantic.

name: 'Vertebración y Desvertebración'
description: These sandstone replicas of a whale spine represent the history and culture of the Canary Islands.

name: Chime Clock at Casa del Arco
description: Ciudad Real's former town hall found new life in 2005 when the chime clock with automatons was installed.

name: Ancient Walls of Valencia
description: Across Valencia lie the remains of three ancient fortifications built centuries apart: a Roman enclosure, an Islamic expansion, and a medieval Christian wall.

name: Hotel Porta Fira
description: This bizarrely tree-like red skyscraper is one of the newer additions to Barcelona’s skyline.

name: Museu de la Pedra en Sec
description: The only museum dedicated entirely to the dry stone walling technique.

name: Tower of the Scipios
description: An elaborate Roman funerary monument dedicated to the family of Scipio.

name: Castillo de Iscar (Iscar Castle)
description: This Castilian castle has stood the test of time like few others.

name: The Bridge Behind the Town and the Tax Collector's Cave
description: On market days, a tax collector sat in a small cave at the end of this arched bridge.

name: Monument to the Eagle of Toledo
description: An homage to the first Spanish cyclist to win the Tour de France.

name: Catafalque of La Torre de Esteban Hambrán
description: You can only see this Baroque masterpiece during the month of November.

name: Estatua de Maruxaina
description: This mermaid sculpture commemorates a Galician legend and an annual trial.

name: Ulpiano Checa Museum
description: Dedicated to one of Spain’s most talented painters and also one of the least known.

name: Fountain of the Unbelievers
description: The engineer of the Imperial Canal of Aragon installed this fountain to show skeptics he had achieved his aim.

name: Puerta de Toledo
description: A masterpiece of Gothic-Mudejar design in Ciudad Real.

name: Valdivia Plum
description: A tribute to the region's fruit industry.

name: Playa de Güigüí
description: Remote beach reachable only by boat or a strenuous hike across a mountain.

name: Elogio del Horizonte
description: This modern sculpture looks out over the sea and amplifies sound from the inside.

name: Aguas de Busot Spa
description: Once a spa resort, then a tuberculosis sanatorium, and now a crumbling ruin.

name: Museo Etnográfico del Silo
description: Go underground to find a collection representing the history of this village in the heart of La Mancha.

name: Farmacia del Puente Mosaics
description: This century-old pharmacy is home to several striking mosaics depicting Catholic saints.

name: Paseo del Sistema Solar
description: A scale model of our Solar System, where physical representations of the Sun and planets have been placed along a route in the local park.

name: El Pozo de Los Padrones (Well of Los Padrones)
description: A well drilled into the side of a cliff supplies most of the water consumed on the island of El Hierro.

name: Guajara Observatory
description: The ruins of the first mountaintop observatory are just a hike away.

name: Barandales Statue
description: Maybe the most iconic character in the Easter (Holy Week) processions in Zamora.

name: ‘13, Rue del Percebe’ Mural
description: Mural dedicated to one of Spain's favorite cartoonists, Francisco Ibáñez.

name: Poblat talaiòtic de Torrellafuda
description: A remarkable prehistoric site featuring well-preserved Talayotic structures offers a glimpse into the ancient civilizations that once thrived on Menorca.

name: Circulo Industrial de Alcoy
description: This local cultural center is housed in one of the most important Valencian Art Nouveau buildings in a town that is blessed with many.

name: Monument to Princess Kristina of Norway
description: Princess Kristina played a pivotal role in fostering diplomatic ties between two kingdoms, Norway and Spain.

name: Museo de Falúas Reales (Royal Barges Museum)
description: A collection of riverboats of the Spanish royal family in a quaint museum.

name: Gallows Hill
description: A former execution site with ornate rock carvings of mysterious origin.

name: Casa dels Gegants
description: Discover everything there is to know about Catalan giants and "Big-Heads."

name: Antonio Machado House-Museum
description: Poet Antonio Machado's former residence offers the quintessential Castilian lifestyle of the provincial towns in the early 20th century.

name: Santiago Padrós Locomotion Mosaic
description: This work of art was conceived as a mosaic originally designed to decorate a car dealership.

name: Museo Casa de Cervantes
description: The Spanish writer devoted hours to writing 'Don Quixote' in this house.

name: Fuente de San Isidro (St. Isidore's Fountain)
description: Legend has it that the water from this fountain has miraculous healing properties.

name: Murales de San Isidro
description: A working-class suburb has become an open-air art museum dedicated to a local poet and hero of the Spanish Civil War.

name: Túnel del Castell
description: An underground passage originally constructed as an air-raid shelter during the Spanish Civil War.

name: Study of Ramón Gómez de la Serna
description: The writer's study, a “portable museum,” is on display at the Museum of Contemporary Art in Madrid.

name: The Tunnel of Bonaparte
description: A tunnel commissioned by ‘the Intruder King’ to escape from the palace if there were problems.

name: Santa Ponsa Quarry
description: Abandoned limestone quarry from the 19th century, now a man-made canyon reclaimed by nature.

name: Torreón de Pinto (Tower of Pinto)
description: A mid-14th-century tower that served as a prison for Spanish nobles.

name: San Blai Snow Cave
description: This human-made cave is one of the many snow storage chambers that supported the ice supply industry in the Sierra Mariola area.

name: Les Adoberies (The Tanneries)
description: This Medieval tannery was accidentally discovered in 2002.

name: Ethnic Music Museum of Busot
description: A private collector gathered these sound machines and musical instruments across five continents.

name: Aranjuez Bullfighting Museum
description: This bullfighting museum is part of a bullfighting coliseum (plaza de toros) built in the 18th century.

name: Museu Diocesà de Menorca
description: Housed in a former convent, this museum has a collection of religious art, artifacts, and scientific objects that offer insights into the island's spiritual, cultural, and intellectual heritage.

name: Roman Altars and Tombstones
description: For centuries, one side of this basilica has integrated local archaeological finds.

name: Provincial Museum of the Sea
description: Exploring the history of San Cibrao’s coastal life and whaling industry.

name: Ojos al Cielo
description: This astronomical visitor center is a wonderful mixture of science and art.

name: Pajarera del Jardín de la Isla
description: A massive birdcage in this royal, 18th-century garden has sparked a local controversy.

name: Three Pieces of the Berlin Wall
description: Part of the wall is preserved in the aptly named Parque de Berlín in Madrid.

name: Arc de l'Aigua (Arch of the Water)
description: This decorative arch symbolizes the history of this medieval village but still maintains a modern purpose.

name: SS American Star Propeller Hub
description: This propeller hub is one of the last remnants of a ship that crashed off the coast of the Canary Islands.

name: Atis Tirma Monument
description: Memorial to the brave indigenous defenders of the Canary Islands.

name: Murals of Penelles
description: This tiny village in Catalonia reinvented itself as a street-art destination.

name: La Regenta
description: A statue honoring the tragic heroine of a Spanish literary masterpiece.

name: Entremontes Winery’s Tile Mosaics
description: Cervantes’ Don Quixote lives on in mosaics at this La Mancha winery.

name: Shrine of La Mare de Déu del Toro
description: The highest point of this Balearic island is home to a historic monastery.

name: Piedra del Concejo
description: This odd stone has served as a meeting place for local councils since the 17th century.

name: Carved Stone Milestones
description: These milestones have survived centuries and still mark distances although incorrectly.

name: Monument als Castellers
description: Few symbols capture the spirit of Catalonia more vividly than its tradition of human towers.

name: Paseo de las Estrellas de la Ciencia
description: The only open-air "Hall of Fame" for science stars in the world.

name: Llorenç Cerdá Bisbal Statue
description: This iconic statue pays tribute to a renowned local artist.

name: Antarctica Roundabout
description: This roundabout commemorates the fourth International Polar Year.

name: La Carbonería
description: One of the most photographed buildings in a city known for its Modernista architecture is an abandoned squat.

name: The Giant of Extremadura
description: The lengthy skeleton of one of the tallest Spaniards of all time is on display in Madrid.

name: The Pools of Castellnou
description: Cursed swimming pool left abandoned is now a favorite of filmmakers and ghost hunters.

name: Lo Pinyol
description: Read a book with a glass of wine at this classically restored bar.

name: Cortijo Jurado
description: This abandoned mansion has a window for every day of the year, and is a temple for ghost hunters.

name: Sitges Aquatic Paradise
description: Abandoned waterpark turned counter-culture mecca.

name: Bosque Animado de Oma
description: A living art gallery painted on a canvas of Monterey pine trees.

name: Mas de la Muga
description: Hidden well off the beaten path in Catalonia is this crumbling medieval castle that is slowly being covered in graffiti.

name: Carabanchel Prison
description: Infamous and now demolished panopticon prison built by its former inhabitants.

name: Herminia's Bakery
description: A pastry shop specializing in Sephardic sweets serves historic recipes in Ribadavia’s Jewish quarter.

name: Besalu Micromundi Museum of Miniatures
description: Medieval village and microminiature museum.

name: Fadrique de Basilea Book Museum
description: A delightful museum devoted to the history of books.

name: Museu del Joguet (Toy Museum of Catalonia)
description: Museum of amazing old toys and automata.

name: Fideus de Vermar
description: Gargantuan pots of slow-cooked lamb, noodles, and red wine feed everyone at a Mallorcan harvest festival.
